part_title: Section Twenty-Three: Critical Errors Leading to Failure of Integrated Pest Management Programmes
part_short_title: Critical Errors Leading to Failure of Integrated Pest Management Programmes
part_subtitle: Critical Errors Leading to Failure of Tuta absoluta IPM Programmes

items.text: Chapter Ten
Integrated Management of Tuta absoluta
Introduction

Tuta absoluta management programmes do not always fail because insecticides are unavailable or ineffective. Many failures begin with a small error in the nursery, a short delay in inspection, or an inaccurate trap reading, after which errors accumulate until larvae reach the fruits and correction becomes more difficult and costly.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta management programmes do not always fail because insecticides are unavailable or ineffective. Many failures begin with a small error in the nursery, a short delay in inspection, or an inaccurate trap reading, after which errors accumulate until larvae reach the fruits and correction becomes more difficult and costly.

items.text: Tuta absoluta is a pest that strongly penalises sequential mistakes because:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta is a pest that strongly penalises sequential mistakes because:

items: Eggs are small and difficult to detect.
Insect stages overlap within the field.
Adults can move among crop cycles.
The pest can develop resistance to insecticides.
Limited fruit infestation may cause major marketing losses.
Old fields may reinvade newly planted fields.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Eggs are small and difficult to detect.
[[prepared]]The pest can develop resistance to insecticides.
[[prepared]]Limited fruit infestation may cause major marketing losses.
[[prepared]]Old fields may reinvade newly planted fields.

items.text: One error may act as the primary cause, while others increase its severity.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One error may act as the primary cause, while others increase its severity.

items.text: For example:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]For example:

items: 4. Larvae are detected only after reaching L3 and L4.
5. An insecticide unsuitable for the instar is used.
6. The same group is repeated when performance is weak.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]4. Larvae are detected only after reaching L3 and L4.
[[prepared]]5. An insecticide unsuitable for the instar is used.
[[prepared]]6. The same group is repeated when performance is weak.

items.text: Failure should therefore be analysed as a causal chain, not as an isolated incident.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Failure should therefore be analysed as a causal chain, not as an isolated incident.

items.text: Definition of a Critical Error
A critical error is an action or omission that can, alone or in combination with other errors, lead to one or more of the following outcomes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A critical error is an action or omission that can, alone or in combination with other errors, lead to one or more of the following outcomes:

items: Establishment of the pest within the field.
Loss of the window for targeting eggs and young larvae.
Violation of preharvest intervals or residue limits.
Increased control cost without a return.
Transformation of the field into a regional infestation source.
Loss of a substantial proportion of marketable yield.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Establishment of the pest within the field.
[[prepared]]Loss of the window for targeting eggs and young larvae.
[[prepared]]Violation of preharvest intervals or residue limits.
[[prepared]]Transformation of the field into a regional infestation source.
[[prepared]]Loss of a substantial proportion of [[term:marketable-yield]]marketable yield[[/term]].

items.text: Not every error necessarily causes complete failure on every occasion. Some errors may pass without obvious damage during a low-pressure season and cause severe loss in another season.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not every error necessarily causes complete failure on every occasion. Some errors may pass without obvious damage during a low-pressure season and cause severe loss in another season.

items.text: Classification of Errors by Severity
Catastrophic Errors
These can cause programme collapse or wide-scale spread of the pest, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These can cause programme collapse or wide-scale spread of the pest, such as:

items: Distributing infested transplants to several fields.
Leaving a heavily infested field without termination.
Repeating an insecticide group that has shown reduced performance.
Using an unregistered material close to harvest.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Leaving a heavily infested field without termination.
[[prepared]]Repeating an insecticide group that has shown reduced performance.
[[prepared]]Using an unregistered material close to harvest.

items.text: High-Risk Errors
These greatly reduce the probability of success, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These greatly reduce the probability of success, such as:

items.text: Moderate-Risk Errors
These may not cause failure alone, but they weaken the programme, such as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]These may not cause failure alone, but they weaken the programme, such as:

items: Failing to link inspection frequency to temperature.
Poor documentation of the biological belt.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Failing to link inspection frequency to temperature.

items.text: The risk associated with a moderate error increases when it is repeated or coincides with high pest pressure.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The risk associated with a moderate error increases when it is repeated or coincides with high pest pressure.

One of the most dangerous practices is to regard the Tuta programme as a response that begins when mines appear.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This causes loss of opportunities to:

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Larvae may already be inside the plant before the team begins preparing the programme.

The programme should begin before transplanting through:

items.text: Error Three: Failing to Define Responsibilities
Good data may be collected without a decision being issued because:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Good data may be collected without a decision being issued because:

items: The trap reader does not communicate with the crop-protection engineer.
The spray operator does not know the target instar.
The harvest officer does not know the preharvest interval.
The nursery fails to report a suspected infestation.
An old field is removed without notifying neighbouring fields.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]The trap reader does not communicate with the crop-protection engineer.
[[prepared]]The spray operator does not know the target instar.
[[prepared]]The harvest officer does not know the preharvest interval.
[[prepared]]The nursery fails to report a suspected infestation.
[[prepared]]An old field is removed without notifying neighbouring fields.

Without data from previous seasons, the team does not know:

Which materials have shown reduced efficacy.

items.text: Second: Nursery and Transplant Errors
Error Five: Accepting Infested Transplants
Moving infested transplants is one of the fastest routes for establishing the pest within a field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Moving infested transplants is one of the fastest routes for establishing the pest within a field.

items.text: A transplant may carry:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A transplant may carry:

items: A small larva within a limited mine.
A pupa in plant debris or surrounding substrate.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A small larva within a limited mine.
[[prepared]]A [[term:pupation]]pupa[[/term]] in plant debris or surrounding substrate.

items.text: Distributing an infested batch creates multiple hotspots whose source later becomes difficult to trace.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Distributing an infested batch creates multiple hotspots whose source later becomes difficult to trace.

items.text: Corrective Action

items: Inspect a representative sample from each batch.
Inspect trays, not only individual transplants.
Reject the batch when infestation is widespread.
Trace the source of every batch within the field.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Inspect a representative sample from each batch.
[[prepared]]Reject the batch when infestation is widespread.
[[prepared]]Trace the source of every batch within the field.

items.text: Error Six: Spraying Infested Transplants and Moving Them Immediately
Spraying may kill some stages, but it does not guarantee:

prepared_items: [[prepared]]Entry of the material into all mines.

items.text: It may also begin an IRAC sequence in the nursery without being recorded within the field programme.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It may also begin an IRAC sequence in the nursery without being recorded within the field programme.

items.text: Corrective Action
After any corrective treatment:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]After any corrective treatment:

items: Confirm that new mines have stopped appearing.
Record the active ingredient and IRAC group.
Do not move the batch until evaluation is complete.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Confirm that new mines have stopped appearing.
[[prepared]]Do not move the batch until evaluation is complete.

items.text: Error Seven: Leaving Nursery Rejects Near Greenhouses or Trays
Rejected plants may allow the pest to complete its life cycle and return to healthy transplants.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rejected plants may allow the pest to complete its life cycle and return to healthy transplants.

items.text: Corrective Action

items: Dispose of them in a manner that prevents insect escape.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Dispose of them in a manner that prevents insect escape.

Transplants may reach the field after the pest has been exposed to several insecticide groups, after which the field uses the same group again.

items.text: Sixth: Cultural and Mechanical-Control Errors
Error Twenty-Six: Leaving Infested Fruits and Plant Parts in the Field

items.text: Eighth: Biological-Control Errors
Error Thirty-Four: Releasing Natural Enemies After Infestation Has Advanced
Egg parasitoids provide no benefit when eggs are absent, and a small number of predators cannot repair a crop whose fruits are already infested.

items.text: Eleventh: Insecticide and Resistance-Management Errors
Error Fifty: Selecting an Insecticide by Trade Name
Different trade names may contain the same active ingredient or the same mode-of-action group.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Different trade names may contain the same active ingredient or the same mode-of-action group.

items.text: Error Fifty-One: Repeating an IRAC Group
This is one of the most serious causes of resistance, especially when successive generations are exposed to the same group.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This is one of the most serious causes of resistance, especially when successive generations are exposed to the same group.

items.text: Corrective Action

items: Avoid repeating the group in the following generation.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoid repeating the group in the following generation.

items.text: Error Fifty-Two: Treating a Change of Active Ingredient Within the Same Group as Rotation
The two active ingredients may share the same target site, so selection pressure continues.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The two active ingredients may share the same target site, so selection pressure continues.

items.text: Corrective Action
Rotate among genuinely different groups while reviewing the potential for cross-resistance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Rotate among genuinely different groups while reviewing the potential for cross-resistance.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use a mixture only when it is:

items: Not unnecessarily duplicating modes of action.

items.text: Error Fifty-Five: Preventive Chemical Spraying Without Demonstrated Risk
This consumes effective groups and kills beneficial organisms before they are needed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This consumes effective groups and kills beneficial organisms before they are needed.

Reserve chemical insecticides for situations in which the following are demonstrated:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Reserve chemical insecticides for situations in which the following are demonstrated:

items.text: Error Fifty-Six: Using a Material Against an Unsuitable Instar
An active ingredient may be effective, but applying it after larvae have entered fruits produces a weak result.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An active ingredient may be effective, but applying it after larvae have entered fruits produces a weak result.

items.text: Corrective Action
Determine larval instar before issuing the spray order.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Determine larval instar before issuing the spray order.

items.text: Error Fifty-Seven: Failing to Include Sprays Against Other Pests
Tuta may be exposed to an IRAC group used against a leaf-feeding caterpillar or another pest, causing repeated pressure that is absent from the Tuta record.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta may be exposed to an IRAC group used against a leaf-feeding caterpillar or another pest, causing repeated pressure that is absent from the Tuta record.

items.text: Corrective Action
Record all insecticide applications used in the field within the comprehensive resistance-management plan.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Record all insecticide applications used in the field within the comprehensive resistance-management plan.

items.text: Error Fifty-Eight: Neglecting the Preharvest Interval
An insecticide may perform well biologically, yet the crop may be rejected because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An insecticide may perform well biologically, yet the crop may be rejected because of:

items: Use of a material not accepted by the factory.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Use of a material not accepted by the factory.

items.text: Corrective Action
Before every decision, review:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before every decision, review:

items.text: Twelfth: Spray-Application Errors
Error Fifty-Nine: Failing to Calibrate the Sprayer
This may produce:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may produce:

items.text: Corrective Action
Measure:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measure:

items.text: Error Sixty: Keeping Water Volume Constant as Plant Size Changes
The water volume used immediately after transplanting is insufficient to cover a dense fruiting crop.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The water volume used immediately after transplanting is insufficient to cover a dense fruiting crop.

items.text: Corrective Action
Recalibrate as:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Recalibrate as:

items: The number of leaf layers increases.

items.text: Error Sixty-One: Focusing on the Upper Leaf Surface
Many eggs, larvae, and predators occur on lower leaf surfaces and within the canopy.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Many eggs, larvae, and predators occur on lower leaf surfaces and within the canopy.

items.text: Corrective Action
Adjust:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Adjust:

items.text: Error Sixty-Two: Spraying Under Unsuitable Wind or Temperature
This causes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This causes:

items.text: Corrective Action
Select a suitable application window and record weather conditions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Select a suitable application window and record weather conditions.

items.text: Error Sixty-Three: Neglecting Spray-Water Quality
The following may affect the stability or efficacy of some products:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The following may affect the stability or efficacy of some products:

items.text: Corrective Action
Measure water quality and use permitted conditioners when required.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measure water quality and use permitted conditioners when required.

items.text: Error Sixty-Four: Preparing the Spray Solution and Leaving It for a Long Period
The efficacy of some active ingredients or biological organisms may decline while remaining in the tank.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The efficacy of some active ingredients or biological organisms may decline while remaining in the tank.

items.text: Corrective Action

items: Do not store diluted spray solution.

items.text: Thirteenth: Post-Intervention Evaluation Errors
Error Sixty-Five: Evaluating the Treatment Too Early
There may not have been sufficient time for the effects of the following to appear:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]There may not have been sufficient time for the effects of the following to appear:

items.text: Corrective Action
Set the assessment date in advance according to mode of action.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Set the assessment date in advance according to mode of action.

items.text: Error Sixty-Six: Evaluating the Treatment Too Late
The treatment outcome may become confounded with:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The treatment outcome may become confounded with:

items.text: Corrective Action
Use more than one assessment date where necessary:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use more than one assessment date where necessary:

items.text: Error Sixty-Seven: Using Total Mine Number as the Success Indicator
Mines remain after larvae die.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Mines remain after larvae die.

items.text: Corrective Action
Measure:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measure:

items.text: Error Sixty-Eight: Declaring Resistance After One Treatment Failure
The cause may be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The cause may be:

items.text: Corrective Action
Conduct a full investigation and a standardised susceptibility test.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conduct a full investigation and a standardised susceptibility test.

items.text: Fourteenth: Harvest and Fruit-Management Errors
Error Sixty-Nine: Leaving Infested Fruits During Harvest
Larvae continue developing within fruits and may reach collection areas or the factory.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Larvae continue developing within fruits and may reach collection areas or the factory.

items.text: Corrective Action

items: Do not throw them between rows.

items.text: Error Seventy: Stopping Monitoring When Harvest Begins
A fresh-market field may remain in production for weeks, while a processing field may remain a source until final mechanical harvest.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A fresh-market field may remain in production for weeks, while a processing field may remain a source until final mechanical harvest.

items.text: Corrective Action
Continue:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Continue:

items.text: Error Seventy-One: Delaying Harvest Without Analysis
Delay may cause:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Delay may cause:

items: Transformation of the field into a source.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Transformation of the field into a source.

items.text: Corrective Action
Link the delay decision to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Link the delay decision to:

items.text: Error Seventy-Two: Combining Rejection Causes
All damaged fruits may be attributed to Tuta despite the presence of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]All damaged fruits may be attributed to Tuta despite the presence of:

items.text: Corrective Action
Classify rejection by cause and separate confirmed Tuta infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Classify rejection by cause and separate confirmed Tuta infestation.

items.text: Fifteenth: Field-Termination and Postharvest Errors
Error Seventy-Three: Stopping Pest Management Before Field Termination
Some growers stop managing the pest after the final economic harvest while plants remain alive for a long period.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Some growers stop managing the pest after the final economic harvest while plants remain alive for a long period.

items.text: Corrective Action
Continue containment until:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Continue containment until:

items: Emergence of a new generation is prevented.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Emergence of a new generation is prevented.

items.text: Error Seventy-Four: Allowing the Field to Dry Naturally
Gradual drying does not kill all stages, and moths may leave for other fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Gradual drying does not kill all stages, and moths may leave for other fields.

items.text: Corrective Action
Use rapid, organised termination rather than waiting for natural plant death.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use rapid, organised termination rather than waiting for natural plant death.

items.text: Error Seventy-Five: Shredding Plants and Leaving Fruits Behind
Larvae may continue within fallen fruits even after the canopy is destroyed.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Larvae may continue within fallen fruits even after the canopy is destroyed.

items.text: Corrective Action
Collect fruits before or during shredding and dispose of them safely.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Collect fruits before or during shredding and dispose of them safely.

items.text: Error Seventy-Six: Failing to Clean Harvesting and Shredding Machinery
Equipment may transport infested residues into the next field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Equipment may transport infested residues into the next field.

items.text: Corrective Action
Clean equipment before movement, recording:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Clean equipment before movement, recording:

items.text: Error Seventy-Seven: Failing to Monitor Regrowth
Plants may regrow after irrigation or moisture and become pest hosts.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Plants may regrow after irrigation or moisture and become pest hosts.

items.text: Corrective Action
Revisit the field after termination and remove any new growth.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Revisit the field after termination and remove any new growth.

items.text: Sixteenth: Area-Wide-Management Errors
Error Seventy-Eight: Managing Each Field as an Isolated Island
Moths can move among fields, causing excellent management in one field to fail because of neighbouring fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Moths can move among fields, causing excellent management in one field to fail because of neighbouring fields.

items.text: Corrective Action
Coordinate:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Coordinate:

items.text: Error Seventy-Nine: Failing to Share Information on Failure and Resistance
Concealing reduced performance of an insecticide group causes continued use in the region and increased resistance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Concealing reduced performance of an insecticide group causes continued use in the region and increased resistance.

items.text: Corrective Action
Establish a reliable technical system for sharing:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Establish a reliable technical system for sharing:

items.text: Error Eighty: Fundamentally Different Programmes Among Neighbours
One field may use a biological programme, while the adjacent field applies broad-spectrum insecticides or leaves residues unmanaged.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One field may use a biological programme, while the adjacent field applies broad-spectrum insecticides or leaves residues unmanaged.

items.text: Corrective Action
Develop a shared minimum standard covering:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Develop a shared minimum standard covering:

items.text: Error Eighty-One: Ignoring Sorting Stations and Factories
Infested fruits and residues may accumulate near collection areas.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested fruits and residues may accumulate near collection areas.

items.text: Corrective Action

items: Prevent host plants around the site.

items.text: Seventeenth: Economic-Evaluation and Administrative Errors
Error Eighty-Two: Evaluating the Programme by Insect Numbers Alone
Pest density may decline without increasing accepted yield, or yield may increase despite persistence of some mines.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Pest density may decline without increasing accepted yield, or yield may increase despite persistence of some mines.

items.text: Corrective Action
Measure:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measure:

items.text: Error Eighty-Three: Ignoring Labour Costs
The biological belt, fruit collection, or release programme may appear inexpensive when labour is not included.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The biological belt, fruit collection, or release programme may appear inexpensive when labour is not included.

items.text: Corrective Action
Include all costs, even permanent farm labour.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Include all costs, even permanent farm labour.

items.text: Error Eighty-Four: Treating the Lowest Number of Sprays as Success
Reducing sprays is not an independent objective when it causes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Reducing sprays is not an independent objective when it causes:

items: Transformation of the field into a source.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Transformation of the field into a source.

items.text: Corrective Action
Measure success through balance among:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Measure success through balance among:

items.text: Error Eighty-Five: Treating the Highest Kill Percentage as the Best Programme
A material may produce rapid mortality but also:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A material may produce rapid mortality but also:

items.text: Corrective Action
Use multi-criteria evaluation covering short- and long-term outcomes.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Use multi-criteria evaluation covering short- and long-term outcomes.

items.text: Error Eighty-Six: Failing to Analyse Causes of Failure
At the end of the season, the conclusion may simply be:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]At the end of the season, the conclusion may simply be:

items.text: “Tuta pressure was high this year.”
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]“Tuta pressure was high this year.”

items.text: Without determining:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Without determining:

items.text: Corrective Action
Conduct an end-of-season review linking every result to a causal chain.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Conduct an end-of-season review linking every result to a causal chain.

items.text: Error Eighty-Seven: Failing to Document a Non-Intervention Decision
Not spraying may be correct, but lack of documentation makes it appear to be neglect.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Not spraying may be correct, but lack of documentation makes it appear to be neglect.

items.text: Corrective Action
Record:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Record:

items.text: Error Eighty-Eight: Failing to Convert Experience into a Written Procedure
An experienced engineer may know when to intervene, but that knowledge is lost when the team changes.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]An experienced engineer may know when to intervene, but that knowledge is lost when the team changes.

items.text: Corrective Action
Convert experience into:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Convert experience into:

items.text: The Ten Highest-Risk Errors
The most operationally serious errors may be ranked as follows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The most operationally serious errors may be ranked as follows:

items: 2. Leaving old fields after they cease to be economically viable.
3. Beginning control only after larvae reach fruits.
6. Killing natural enemies with broad-spectrum insecticides.
9. Failing to manage immigration waves among fields.
10. Ignoring preharvest intervals and residues close to harvest.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]2. Leaving old fields after they cease to be economically viable.
[[prepared]]3. Beginning control only after larvae reach fruits.
[[prepared]]6. Killing natural enemies with broad-spectrum insecticides.
[[prepared]]9. Failing to manage immigration waves among fields.
[[prepared]]10. Ignoring preharvest intervals and residues close to harvest.

items.text: This ranking does not mean that other errors are unimportant. Any error may become critical under specific field conditions.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This ranking does not mean that other errors are unimportant. Any error may become critical under specific field conditions.

items.text: The Most Common Failure Chain
This may be represented as follows:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This may be represented as follows:

items.text: Weak prevention ← absence of early monitoring ← delayed detection ← advanced larval instars ← unsuitable chemical intervention ← weak result ← repetition or mixing of insecticides ← mortality of beneficial organisms ← resistance and resurgence ← fruit infestation ← increased rejection and cost
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Weak prevention ← absence of early monitoring ← delayed detection ← advanced larval instars ← unsuitable chemical intervention ← weak result ← repetition or mixing of insecticides ← mortality of beneficial organisms ← resistance and resurgence ← fruit infestation ← increased rejection and cost

items.text: The chain should be broken at the earliest possible point rather than at its end.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The chain should be broken at the earliest possible point rather than at its end.

items.text: The Corresponding Success Chain

Healthy transplants ← traps and early inspection ← egg detection ← protection and augmentation of parasitoids ← targeting L1 and L2 ← hotspot treatment ← genuine rotation of IRAC groups ← fruit protection ← organised harvest ← rapid field termination
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Healthy transplants ← traps and early inspection ← egg detection ← protection and augmentation of parasitoids ← targeting L1 and L2 ← hotspot treatment ← genuine rotation of IRAC groups ← fruit protection ← organised harvest ← rapid field termination

items.text: Each step reduces the burden on the following step.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Each step reduces the burden on the following step.

items.text: Root-Cause Analysis
When failure occurs, use the following sequence.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When failure occurs, use the following sequence.

items.text: What Was the Final Problem?
Example: Increased fruit infestation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Example: Increased fruit infestation.

items.text: Why Did It Occur?
Because advanced larvae reached the clusters.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Because advanced larvae reached the clusters.

items.text: Why Did Larvae Reach Advanced Instars?
Because the hatching wave was not detected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Because the hatching wave was not detected.

items.text: Why Was It Not Detected?
Because inspection remained weekly despite rising temperature, and field edges were not inspected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Because inspection remained weekly despite rising temperature, and field edges were not inspected.

items.text: Why Were Edges Not Inspected?
Because sectors were not defined and responsibility was unclear.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Because sectors were not defined and responsibility was unclear.

items.text: The root cause is therefore administrative and related to monitoring, not simply weak insecticide performance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The root cause is therefore administrative and related to monitoring, not simply weak insecticide performance.

items.text: Error and Corrective-Action Matrix

rows: Premature evaluation of a biological product

items.text: Error-Prevention System
The system is built on four layers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system is built on four layers.

items.text: Layer One: Prevention
Layer Two: Detection
Layer Three: Correction
Layer Four: Prevention of Recurrence
Checklist Before Any Intervention
Before issuing a control order, confirm:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Before issuing a control order, confirm:

items: Review of the most recent IRAC group.
Confirmation that the sprayer is calibrated.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Review of the most recent IRAC group.

items.text: When these data are absent and no severe emergency exists, they should be completed before intervention.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]When these data are absent and no severe emergency exists, they should be completed before intervention.

items.text: Checklist After Intervention
Review:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Review:

items.text: Culture of Error Reporting
Errors and failure cases should not be concealed out of fear of blame because concealment causes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Errors and failure cases should not be concealed out of fear of blame because concealment causes:

items.text: The system should focus on:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should focus on:

items: Defining professional responsibility without discouraging early reporting.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Defining professional responsibility without discouraging early reporting.

items.text: Applied Priorities in Egypt

items: 3. Standardise the definition of an active mine.
4. Train engineers to identify larval instars.
5. Include fruit infestation in every inspection after fruit set.
6. Require projects to maintain an IRAC record.
7. Record nursery sprays and treatments against other pests.
9. Prevent rate increases and random mixtures.
11. Develop a plan for removing old fields.
13. Organise waste from sorting stations and factories.
17. Measure accepted yield, not total yield alone.
19. Conduct root-cause analysis at the end of the season.
20. Convert recurring errors into administrative performance indicators.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]3. Standardise the definition of an active mine.
[[prepared]]4. Train engineers to identify larval instars.
[[prepared]]5. Include fruit infestation in every inspection after fruit set.
[[prepared]]6. Require projects to maintain an IRAC record.
[[prepared]]7. Record nursery sprays and treatments against other pests.
[[prepared]]9. Prevent rate increases and random mixtures.
[[prepared]]11. Develop a plan for removing old fields.
[[prepared]]13. Organise waste from sorting stations and factories.
[[prepared]]17. Measure accepted yield, not total yield alone.
[[prepared]]19. Conduct root-cause analysis at the end of the season.
[[prepared]]20. Convert recurring errors into administrative performance indicators.

items.text: Scientific Summary of Section Twenty-Three
Failure of a Tuta absoluta management programme rarely results from one cause. It usually arises from a chain of errors beginning before visible damage appears.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Failure of a Tuta absoluta management programme rarely results from one cause. It usually arises from a chain of errors beginning before visible damage appears.

items.text: The most serious failure points include:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The most serious failure points include:

items: Relying on traps without inspecting plants.

items.text: A timing error cannot be corrected by increasing the insecticide rate. Once a larva enters a fruit, it becomes more difficult to target, and treatment cannot reverse damage already caused.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A timing error cannot be corrected by increasing the insecticide rate. Once a larva enters a fruit, it becomes more difficult to target, and treatment cannot reverse damage already caused.

items.text: Changing the trade name is not rotation when the mode of action remains the same. Resistance should not be declared after one failed treatment before reviewing:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Changing the trade name is not rotation when the mode of action remains the same. Resistance should not be declared after one failed treatment before reviewing:

items.text: Biological control requires early initiation, good transport and distribution, and protection from insecticides. Releasing natural enemies without measuring emergence and field establishment is insufficient.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Biological control requires early initiation, good transport and distribution, and protection from insecticides. Releasing natural enemies without measuring emergence and field establishment is insufficient.

items.text: Likewise, a biological belt cannot perform its function when its plants are established without assessment, sprayed directly, or allowed to become sources of other pests.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Likewise, a biological belt cannot perform its function when its plants are established without assessment, sprayed directly, or allowed to become sources of other pests.

items.text: Harvest and postharvest stages are as important as the beginning of the season because infested fruits, old fields, and residues can maintain the pest cycle and transfer it to the following crop.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Harvest and postharvest stages are as important as the beginning of the season because infested fruits, old fields, and residues can maintain the pest cycle and transfer it to the following crop.

items.text: The most efficient programme is the one that prevents the failure chain at its beginning through:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The most efficient programme is the one that prevents the failure chain at its beginning through:
